senses.definition: used to say that something has happened because of somebody/something
examples: It was all a great success—thanks to a lot of hard work.
Thanks to the efforts of the whole team, the project was completed on time.
Prices have edged higher this year, thanks in part to rising exports.
Everyone knows about it now, thanks to you!

senses.definition: to such a degree; so
examples: I can't walk that far (= as far as that).
If they are that close, they will be here in minutes.
If only it were that simple.

senses.definition: used when saying how much or showing how long, big, etc. something is with your hands
examples: It's about that long.

senses.definition: not very, or not as much as has been said
examples: It isn't all that cold.
It's not quite that easy.
It's not really that different in France.
There aren't that many people here.

senses.definition: used to emphasize how much
examples: I was that scared I didn't know what to do.

senses.definition: used after some verbs, adjectives and nouns to introduce a new part of the sentence
examples: She said (that) the story was true.
It’s possible (that) he has not received the letter.
He seemed pleased (that) I wanted to continue.
The fact (that) he’s older than me is not relevant.

senses.definition: used to express a result
examples: She was so tired (that) she couldn’t think straight.

senses.definition: used for expressing a hope or a wish
examples: Oh that I could see him again!

senses.definition: used for referring to a person or thing that is not near the speaker or as near to the speaker as another
examples: Look at that man over there.
How much are those apples at the back?

senses.definition: used for referring to somebody/something that has already been mentioned or is already known about
examples: I was living with my parents at that time.
That incident changed their lives.
Have you forgotten about that money I lent you last week?
That dress of hers is too short.

senses.definition: used for referring to a person or thing that is not near the speaker, or not as near to the speaker as another
examples: Who's that?
That's Peter over there.
Hello. Is that Jo? (= when speaking on the phone)
That's a nice dress.
Those look riper than these.

senses.definition: used for referring to somebody/something that has already been mentioned, or is already known about
examples: What can I do about that?
Do you remember when we went to Norway? That was a good trip.
That's exactly what I think.

senses.definition: used for referring to people or things of a particular type
examples: Those present were in favour of change.
There are those who say (= some people say) she should not have got the job.
Salaries are higher here than those in my country.

senses.definition: used as a relative pronoun to introduce a part of a sentence which refers to the person, thing or time you have been talking about
examples: Where's the letter that came yesterday?
Who was it that won the US Open?
The watch (that) you gave me keeps perfect time.
The people (that) I spoke to were very helpful.
It’s the best novel (that) I’ve ever read.

senses.definition: and everything else connected with an activity, a situation, etc.
examples: Did you bring the contract and (all) that?

senses.definition: used to say what something means or to give more information
examples: He's a local government administrator, that is to say a civil servant.
You'll find her very helpful—if she's not too busy, that is.

senses.definition: used to say that somebody is right, or is doing something right
examples: No, the other one… that’s it.
That's it, carry on!

senses.definition: used to say that something is finished, or that no more can be done
examples: That's it, the fire's out now.
That's it for now, but if I get any news I'll let you know.
A week to go, and that's it!

senses.definition: used to say that you will not accept something any longer
examples: That's it, I've had enough!

senses.definition: used to talk about the reason for something
examples: So that's it—the fuse had gone.
You don't love me any more, is that it?

senses.definition: used to say that your decision cannot be changed
examples: Well I'm not going, and that's that.

senses.definition: used to refer to somebody/something that has already been mentioned or is easily understood
examples: There were three questions. The first two were relatively easy but the third one was hard.
There was an accident here yesterday. A car hit a tree and the driver was killed.
The heat was getting to be too much for me.
The nights are getting longer.

senses.definition: used to refer to somebody/something that is the only, normal or obvious one of their kind
examples: the Mona Lisa
the Nile
the Queen
What's the matter?
The phone rang.
I patted her on the back.
How's the (= your) baby?

senses.definition: used when explaining which person or thing you mean
examples: the house at the end of the street
The people I met there were very friendly.
It was the best day of my life.
You're the third person to ask me that.
Friday the thirteenth
Alexander the Great

senses.definition: used to refer to a thing in general rather than a particular example
examples: He taught himself to play the violin.
The dolphin is an intelligent animal.
They placed the African elephant on their endangered list.
I heard it on the radio.
I'm usually out during the day.

senses.definition: used with adjectives to refer to a thing or a group of people described by the adjective
examples: With him, you should always expect the unexpected.
the unemployed
the French

senses.definition: used before the plural of somebody’s last name to refer to a whole family or a married couple
examples: Don't forget to invite the Jordans.

senses.definition: used with a unit of measurement to mean ‘every’
examples: My car does forty miles to the gallon.

senses.definition: enough of something for a particular purpose
examples: I wanted it but I didn't have the money.

senses.definition: used with a unit of time to mean ‘the present’
examples: Why not have the dish of the day?

senses.definition: used, stressing the, to show that the person or thing referred to is famous or important
examples: Sheryl Crow? Not the Sheryl Crow?

senses.definition: used to show that two things change to the same degree
examples: The more she thought about it, the more depressed she became.
The less said about the whole thing, the happier I'll be.

senses.definition: a building or an outdoor area where plays and similar types of entertainment are performed
examples: Broadway theatres
an open-air theatre

senses.definition: a building in which films are shown
examples: They arrived at the movie theater a few minutes later.

senses.definition: plays considered as entertainment
examples: an evening of live music and theatre

current ideas about what makes good theatre (= what makes good entertainment when performed)

live/street/youth theatre
a theatre critic

senses.definition: the work of writing, producing and acting in plays
examples: I want to work in theatre.
a theatre troupe/company

senses.definition: a room in a hospital used for medical operations
examples: a theatre sister (= a nurse who helps during operations)

senses.definition: the place in which a war or fighting takes place

senses.definition: connected with the theatre

senses.definition: deliberately attracting attention or creating a particular effect in a way that seems false
examples: a theatrical gesture

senses.definition: the crime of stealing something from a person or place

senses.definition: of or belonging to people, animals or things that have already been mentioned or are easily identified
examples: Their parties are always fun.

senses.definition: used instead of his or her to refer to a person whose sex is not mentioned or not known
examples: If anyone calls, ask for their number so I can call them back.

senses.definition: of or belonging to them
examples: Theirs are the children with very fair hair.

senses.definition: used when referring to people, animals or things as the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be

senses.definition: used instead of him or her to refer to a person whose sex is not mentioned or not known
examples: If anyone comes in before I get back, ask them to wait.

senses.definition: the subject or main idea in a talk, piece of writing or work of art

senses.definition: a short tune that is repeated or developed in a piece of music

senses.definition: music that is played at the beginning and end and/or is often repeated in a film, television programme, etc.

senses.definition: a short piece of writing on a particular subject, done for school
the part of a sentence or clause that contains information that is not new to the reader or audience
used when people or animals performing an action are also affected by it
examples: They seemed to be enjoying themselves.

senses.definition: used instead of himself or herself to refer to a person whose sex is not mentioned or not known

senses.definition: used to emphasize they or a plural subject
examples: They themselves had had a similar experience.

senses.definition: alone; without anyone else
examples: They wanted to spend the evening by themselves.

senses.definition: without help
examples: They did the cooking by themselves.

senses.definition: for only them to have or use; not shared with others
used to refer to a particular time in the past or future
examples: Life was harder then because neither of us had a job.

I saw them at Christmas but haven't heard a thing since then.

senses.definition: used to introduce the next item in a series of actions, events, instructions, etc.
examples: He ate a cookie, then another and then another.

senses.definition: used to show the logical result of a particular statement or situation

senses.definition: used to introduce additional information
examples: She's been very busy at work and then there was all that trouble with her son.

senses.definition: used to introduce a summary of something that has just been said
examples: These, then, are the main areas of concern.

senses.definition: used to show the beginning or end of a conversation, statement, etc.
examples: Right then, where do you want the table to go?

senses.definition: used to emphasize the large amount or number of something, and to say that you have not mentioned everything
examples: There are Indian, Chinese, Mexican, Thai restaurants… and then some!

senses.definition: used to introduce additional information or information that contrasts with something that has just been said
examples: She was early, but then again, she always is.

senses.definition: from time to time; occasionally
examples: Every now and then she checked to see if he was still asleep.

senses.definition: used to show in a mild way that you do not approve of something
used when making a suggestion or an offer
examples: Now then, who wants to come for a walk?

senses.definition: the study of religion and beliefs
examples: a degree in theology
a theology student
the rarefied world of academic theology

senses.definition: a set of religious beliefs
examples: the theologies of the East

senses.definition: connected with the ideas and principles on which a particular subject is based, rather than with practice and experiment
examples: a theoretical approach
theoretical physics

senses.definition: that could possibly exist, happen or be true, although this is unlikely
examples: It's a theoretical possibility.

senses.definition: a formal set of ideas that is intended to explain why something happens or exists
examples: the theory of evolution/relativity
scientific/economic theory

senses.definition: the principles on which a particular subject is based
examples: the theory and practice of language teaching

literary theory

senses.definition: an opinion or idea that somebody believes is true but that is not proved
examples: Theories abound (= people have lots of different ideas) about what happened.

senses.definition: a specialist who treats a particular type of illness or problem, or who uses a particular type of treatment
examples: a speech therapist
a beauty therapist

senses.definition: a person who is trained to treat somebody who has problems with their mental health by talking with them
the treatment of a physical problem or an illness
examples: He is receiving therapy for cancer.

alternative/complementary therapies (= treatments that do not use traditional drugs)
new cancer therapies

senses.definition: the treatment of problems with somebody's mental health by talking with them
examples: She's in therapy.
I went into therapy because my doctor suggested it.
the use of hypnosis as a form of therapy
a therapy group/session

senses.definition: used to show that something exists or happens
examples: There's a restaurant around the corner.
There are two people waiting outside.
Has there been an accident?
I don't want there to be any misunderstanding.
There seemed to be no doubt about it.

There comes a point where you give up.
There remains the problem of finance.
There used to be a cinema here.
Suddenly there was a loud bang.
Many people believe there need to be more women in video games.
There's only four days left.
There once was a poor farmer who had four sons.

senses.definition: in, at or to that place or position
examples: We went on to Paris and stayed there eleven days.
I hope we get there in time.
It's there, right in front of you!
There it is—just behind the chair.
We're almost there (= we have almost arrived).
Are we nearly there yet?
Come on, I know you're there somewhere.
Hello, is Bob there please? (= used when calling somebody on the phone)
‘Have you seen my pen?’ ‘Yes, it's over there.’
There are a lot of people back there (= behind) waiting to get in.
I'm not going in there—it's freezing!
I left in 2008 and I haven't been back there since.
Can I get there and back in a day?
I took one look at the car and offered to buy it there and then/then and there (= immediately).
James just sat there in silence.
He wasn't doing anything, just standing there.

senses.definition: existing or available
examples: I went to see if my old school was still there.
The money's there if you need it.
This is the most exciting technology out there today.
Most of the system is already there, just waiting to be used.

senses.definition: at that point (in a story, an argument, etc.)

I don't agree with you there.

senses.definition: used to attract somebody’s attention
examples: Hello, there!

senses.definition: used to attract somebody’s attention to a particular person, thing or fact

senses.definition: used to show the role of a person or thing in a situation

senses.definition: used to show that you think a place or an activity is not very interesting or impressive because you have already experienced it

senses.definition: to be available if somebody wants to talk to you or if they need help
examples: You know I'll always be there for you.

senses.definition: there; to there
examples: He's over by there.

senses.definition: to know all about a situation because you have experienced it

examples: Papers were scattered here and there on the floor.

senses.definition: not intelligent or not able to understand what is happening
used to show that you are determined not to change your attitude or opinion
examples: Well, you can't have it, so there!

senses.definition: that is the situation
examples: It's crazy, I know, but there it is.

senses.definition: used to say that something is very good, even if it is not perfect
examples: At the end of the tournament, he'll be there or thereabouts (= he may not win, but he will be one of the best players).

senses.definition: used to say that something is a very good example of something
examples: She visited him every day he was in the hospital. There's devotion for you.

senses.definition: used to praise or encourage small children or animals
examples: Finish your lunch, there's a good boy.

senses.definition: used to say that something has a particular quality
used to persuade a small child to stop crying or being upset
examples: There, there! Never mind, you'll soon feel better.

senses.definition: used when giving somebody a thing they want or have asked for
examples: There you are—that'll be £3.80, please.
OK, there you go.

senses.definition: used when explaining or showing something to somebody
examples: You switch on, push in the DVD and there you are!

senses.definition: used when you are talking about something that happens in a typical way or about a situation that cannot be changed

senses.definition: after the time or event mentioned

examples: He's only 17 and therefore not eligible to vote.

senses.definition: a long piece of writing completed by a student as part of a university degree, based on their own research
examples: Students must submit a thesis on an agreed subject within four years.

senses.definition: a statement or an opinion that is discussed in a logical way and presented with evidence in order to prove that it is true
examples: The basic thesis of the book is fairly simple.

senses.definition: people, animals or things that have already been mentioned or are easily identified
examples: ‘Where are John and Liz?’ ‘They went for a walk.’

senses.definition: used instead of he or she to refer to a person whose sex is not mentioned or not known
examples: If anyone arrives late they'll have to wait outside.

senses.definition: people in general
examples: The rest, as they say, is history.

senses.definition: people in authority or experts
examples: They cut my water off.

senses.definition: having a larger distance between opposite sides or surfaces than other similar objects or than normal

a thick book (= one that has a lot of pages)
a thick coat (= one made of heavy cloth)
thick fingers

a thick carpet of snow

senses.definition: used to ask about or state the distance between opposite sides or surfaces
examples: How thick are the walls?

senses.definition: growing closely together in large amounts or numbers
examples: thick dark hair

senses.definition: not flowing very easily

senses.definition: difficult to see through; difficult to breathe in

senses.definition: having a large number of people or a large amount of something in one place
examples: The beach was thick with sunbathers.

senses.definition: an offensive way to describe somebody who is slow to learn or understand things
examples: I'm not completely thick, you know.

senses.definition: easily recognized as being from a particular country or area
examples: a thick Brooklyn accent

senses.definition: deep and not as clear as normal, especially because of illness or emotion
examples: His voice was thick with emotion.

senses.definition: very friendly with somebody, especially in a way that makes other people suspect that something wrong, illegal or dishonest is involved in the situation

senses.definition: family relationships are stronger than any others
to hit somebody/be hit on the head as a punishment
examples: You’ll get a thick ear if you’re not careful!

senses.definition: very friendly, especially in a way that makes other people suspect that something wrong, illegal or dishonest is involved in the situation
very stupid
a physical condition in which your head is painful or you cannot think clearly as a result of an illness or of drinking too much alcohol
examples: You’re going to have a thick head in the morning!

senses.definition: used to show that you are annoyed that somebody does not understand something

senses.definition: the ability to accept criticism, offensive remarks, etc. without becoming upset
a person who steals something from another person or place
examples: a car/jewel thief
a gang of thieves

a petty thief (= who steals items of quite low value)

senses.definition: used to say that even criminals have standards of behaviour that they respect
very friendly, especially in a way that makes other people suspect that something wrong, illegal or dishonest is involved in the situation
having a smaller distance between opposite sides or surfaces than other similar objects or than normal
examples: Cut the vegetables into thin strips.

senses.definition: not covered with much fat or muscle
examples: He was tall and thin, with dark hair.

thin legs

senses.definition: not growing closely together or in large amounts
examples: thin grey hair

senses.definition: containing more liquid than is normal or expected
examples: The sauce was thin and tasteless.

senses.definition: fairly easy to see through
examples: They fought their way through where the smoke was thinner.

senses.definition: containing less oxygen than normal
examples: Humans would not be able to survive in the thin atmosphere of the planet.

senses.definition: high and weak
examples: Her thin voice trailed off into silence.

senses.definition: not sincere or enthusiastic
examples: He gave a thin smile.

senses.definition: not very bright
examples: the thin grey light of dawn

senses.definition: of poor quality; without an important quality
examples: a thin excuse (= one that people are not likely to believe)

senses.definition: to be taking a risk

examples: She can’t just have vanished into thin air.

senses.definition: to have many problems or difficulties to deal with; to not be successful
examples: He’s had a thin time of it since losing his job.

senses.definition: from nowhere or nothing, as if by magic
examples: Unfortunately, I can’t just conjure up the money out of thin air!

senses.definition: to try to do so many different things at the same time that you do not do any of them well

senses.definition: if people or things are thick/thin on the ground, there are a lot/not many of them in a place
examples: Customers are thin on the ground at this time of year.

senses.definition: with little or no hair on the head

senses.definition: to begin to become weaker or less acceptable

senses.definition: a fact, an event, a situation or an action; what somebody says or thinks
